layers.name: Configuration & Infrastructure
layers.description: Project configuration, build tools, CI/CD, deployment, documentation, and editor settings

layers.name: Game Data
layers.description: JSON game data definitions and TypeScript data modules defining weapons, armors, perks, enchantments, buffs, damage formulas, and other game mechanics

layers.name: Damage Engine
layers.description: Core damage calculation engine including weapon stat computation, enchantment application, critical hit resolution, defense mitigation, damage type resolution, and the build pipeline that assembles all game data into final damage numbers

layers.name: Constants & Types
layers.description: Game balance constants for damage, buffs, debuffs, defense, cooldowns, healing, and draconic systems; core TypeScript type definitions and interfaces used across the entire application

layers.name: State Management
layers.description: Svelte stores managing build state persistence, derived calculation results, and toast notifications

layers.name: UI Components
layers.description: Svelte components for the damage calculator interface including root orchestrator, damage analysis panels, buff/debuff displays, build saves, modals, filters, reusable UI primitives, entry point, and styles

nodes.name: static.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow that builds and deploys the Vite/SvelteKit project as static content to GitHub Pages. Triggers on pushes to main or manual dispatch, installs Node 24, runs npm build, and deploys the dist artifact.
nodes.languageNotes: YAML GitHub Actions workflow definition with two jobs: build (checkout, setup-node, install, build, upload artifact) and deploy (deploy-pages).

nodes.name: .gitignore
nodes.summary: Ignores all files within the .fallow directory, preventing fallow cache/artifact files from being committed to version control.
nodes.languageNotes: Git ignore syntax; contains a single wildcard pattern '*' to ignore all contents of the directory.

nodes.summary: Binary cache file used by the fallow tool, likely storing computed analysis or metric data for the project.
nodes.languageNotes: Binary format; contents are opaque and machine-generated.

nodes.languageNotes: Binary format; contents are opaque and machine-generated.

nodes.languageNotes: Binary format; contents are opaque and machine-generated.

nodes.name: .fallowrc.json
nodes.summary: Fallow dead-code linter configuration. Defines entry points (src/index.* and src/main.*), sets duplicate detection threshold to minOccurrences: 3, enables unused-dependencies warnings, and specifies ignoreExports for Svelte modal components and constants that are intentionally unused.
nodes.languageNotes: JSONC format with inline comments. Schema-validated against fallow's JSON Schema.

nodes.name: .gitignore
nodes.summary: Git ignore rules. Excludes logs, node_modules, dist, dist-ssr, local env files, and common IDE/editor directories (VSCode, JetBrains, macOS artifacts, Visual Studio).
nodes.languageNotes: Standard gitignore pattern syntax.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Agent context file providing AI agents with project overview, structure, development workflow, testing instructions, and interaction guidelines for the voxlblade-builder codebase. Documents tech stack (Svelte 5, Vite, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, Vitest, pnpm).
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown with embedded code blocks and directory tree diagram.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project README from the Svelte + TypeScript + Vite template. Covers recommended IDE setup, explains why this template was chosen over SvelteKit, technical considerations for type configuration (global.d.ts vs compilerOptions.types), and HMR behavior.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown with inline TypeScript code examples and external links.

nodes.name: fallow-output.json
nodes.summary: Fallow dead-code analysis output (schema v7, v2.102.0). Found 28 total issues: 1 unused file (svelte.config.js), 23 unused exports across src/lib/engine.ts (12), src/data/* (6), src/lib/defense.ts (2), src/lib/types.ts (1), src/lib/utils.ts (1), 2 unused types in src/data/SummonData.ts, and 2 duplicate exports (modalSearch, value). Includes auto-fix actions and next-step recommendations.
nodes.languageNotes: JSON output from fallow CLI. Contains structured issue reports with file paths, line numbers, span offsets, and actionable fix suggestions.

nodes.name: fallow-security.json
nodes.summary: Fallow security analysis output (schema v7, v2.102.0). Found 25 tainted-sink findings: dangerous-html (CWE-79) in src/DidYouMean.svelte, src/SuggestDrop.svelte, and src/App.svelte (22 instances of template.innerHTML assignments), plus 1 dynamic-regex (CWE-1333) in App.svelte. All low severity, none reachable from untrusted sources. 250 unresolved callee sites mostly from dynamic-dispatch.
nodes.languageNotes: JSON security report with CWE identifiers, reachability analysis, blast radius metrics, and dynamic-dispatch call resolution diagnostics.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Vite application entry HTML. Minimal boilerplate with meta charset/viewport, favicon reference (/favicon.svg), page title 'VoxlBlade Builder', a root #app div for Svelte mounting, and a module script loading /src/main.ts.
nodes.languageNotes: Standard HTML5 doctype. Vite processes this as the dev server and build entry point, injecting scripts during bundling.

nodes.name: tsconfig.app.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript configuration for the application source code. Extends @tsconfig/svelte, targets ES2023, uses ESNext modules, enables allowJs/checkJs for mixed JS/TS in .svelte files, includes all .ts/.js/.svelte files under src/.
nodes.languageNotes: JSON5-style config. Uses noEmit (bundler mode), moduleDetection: force, tsBuildInfoFile for incremental builds.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: Root TypeScript configuration. Simply extends tsconfig.app.json with no overrides, serving as the project-level default for IDE and editor integration.
nodes.languageNotes: Minimal 3-line JSON config. Acts as the root tsconfig that editors and tools resolve to by default.

nodes.name: tsconfig.node.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript configuration for Node.js/Vite tooling files. Targets ES2023, uses bundler module resolution, enables strict linting (noUnusedLocals/Parameters, noFallthroughCases), verbatimModuleSyntax, and only includes vite.config.ts.
nodes.languageNotes: Separate tsconfig for build tooling. Uses erasableSyntaxOnly for strip-only transforms, allowImportingTsExtensions for bundler resolution.

nodes.name: vercel.json
nodes.summary: Vercel deployment configuration. Sets build command to 'npm run build', output directory to 'dist', framework to 'vite'. Configures cache headers: immutable 1-year cache for /assets/*, must-revalidate for /sw.js and /index.html.
nodes.languageNotes: JSON config for Vercel platform. Cache-Control headers optimize static asset delivery while ensuring HTML and service worker stay fresh.

nodes.name: armors.json
nodes.summary: Defines ~100+ armor sets for Voxel Blade. Each armor has a name, shared stats (elemental boosts/defenses, physical/magic defense, tenacity, protection, warding, resistances, speed/jump/attack speed), a perk with name and amount, and 1-3 parts (Helmet, Chestplate, Leggings) with descriptions. Covers a wide range of themed sets from basic (Civilian) to endgame (Queen Bumblz, Iron Slayer).
nodes.languageNotes: JSON array of objects; each armor has name, sharedPart (upgrade, stats, perkName, perkAmount), and parts array with type and description.

nodes.name: blades.json
nodes.summary: Defines 107 blade weapons across tiers 1-5. Each blade has name, tier, attackSpeed, stats (damage type weights, scaling values, boost values), optional perks array, and bladeType (Small/Medium/Heavy Blade or Hammer Head). Covers physical, elemental (fire, water, earth, air, hex, holy), and hybrid damage types.
nodes.languageNotes: JSON array; each blade has name, tier, attackSpeed, stats object, optional perks array, and bladeType string.

nodes.name: enchantments.json
nodes.summary: Defines 52 enchantments split into unAscended (25) and Ascended (27) categories. Each has name, category, stacking type (exclusive or multi), stats with modifier operations (multiplier/addition), optional effects array, and additionalNotes. Enchantments modify weapon/armor stats with multiplicative or additive modifiers, and some grant special effects like Lifesteal, Perfection, or Wildfire.
nodes.languageNotes: JSON array; stats use a nested modifier pattern with type (multiplier/addition) and value. Effects have name and value fields.

nodes.name: essences.json
nodes.summary: Defines 106 essences across tiers 1-5. Essences are weapon components that pair with blades and handles. Each has name, tier, attackSpeed, stats (scaling and boost values), optional perks, and essenceType. Mirrors the blade data structure closely, providing the essence component for weapon crafting.
nodes.languageNotes: JSON array; structure similar to blades.json with name, tier, attackSpeed, stats, optional perks, and essenceType.

nodes.name: gloves.json
nodes.summary: Defines 22 gloves across tiers 1-4. Each glove has name, tier, attackSpeed, stats, perks array, and gloveType (Gloves or Shield). Gloves are equipment pieces that provide stats, perks, and some include spirit-based finisher abilities. Includes both offensive and defensive options like Croakernaut Shield.
nodes.languageNotes: JSON array; smaller dataset with name, tier, attackSpeed, stats, perks array, and gloveType.

nodes.name: guilds.json
nodes.summary: Defines 9 guilds (Gladiator, Cursed, Cleric, Scholar, Knight, Thief, Draconic, Monk, Vampire), each with 3 ranks. Each guild has name and ranks array with rank number, stats bonuses, and perks. Guilds provide scaling stat bonuses and perks that improve with rank progression.
nodes.languageNotes: JSON array with nested ranks; each rank has stats and perks arrays.

nodes.name: handles.json
nodes.summary: Defines 104 handles across tiers 1-5. Handles are weapon components that pair with blades and essences. Each has name, tier, attackSpeed, stats (scaling and boost values), optional perks, and handleType (Medium Handle, Long Handle, or Pole). Handles determine weapon reach and provide additional perks and stats.
nodes.languageNotes: JSON array; structure similar to blades/essences with name, tier, attackSpeed, stats, optional perks, and handleType.

nodes.name: perks.json
nodes.summary: Defines 365 perks with names, descriptions, and tags. Each perk has a name, detailed description of its mechanic, and tags array classifying it (e.g., Offensive, Defensive, Armor, Weapon, Rune, Buff, Debuff, Finisher, etc.). This is the central reference for all perk effects used throughout the calculator.
nodes.languageNotes: JSON array at 3689 lines; each perk has name, description string, and tags array of strings.

nodes.name: races.json
nodes.summary: Defines 10 playable races (Human, Half-Ork, Elf, Dark Elf, Dragon Blooded, Ork, High Elf, Arborian, Kitsune, Bunikin). Each race has a name and passive description, with some having statModifiers, cooldownModifiers, or special penetration values. Races provide unique passive bonuses that affect gameplay.
nodes.languageNotes: JSON array; each race has name, passive text, and optional statModifiers/cooldownModifiers/special values.

nodes.name: rings.json
nodes.summary: Defines 35 rings. Each ring has name, description, upgrade level, stats, perkName, and perkAmount. Rings are equipment accessories that provide stat bonuses and perks. Covers various builds: offensive (Sharp Ring, Fire Ring), defensive (Bastion Ring, Parrying Ring), utility (Quickstep Ring, Lucky Ring), and summon-focused (Bumblz Honey Ring).
nodes.languageNotes: JSON array; each ring has name, description, upgrade, stats, perkName, perkAmount.

nodes.name: runes.json
nodes.summary: Defines 41 runes (active abilities). Each rune has name, description, upgrade level, stats, cooldown, and optional perkName/perkAmount. Runes provide active skills like Fireball, Heal, Combat Roll, Summon mounts, and various offensive/defensive abilities. Each has a cooldown in seconds.
nodes.languageNotes: JSON array; each rune has name, description, upgrade, stats, cooldown number, and optional perk fields.

nodes.name: .slim/deepwork/ui-upgrade.md
nodes.summary: UI upgrade plan document outlining 8 phases of refactoring for the Voxel Blade calculator: extracting modal system, adding toast notifications, CSS consolidation, responsive improvements, performance optimization, accessibility, and build comparison. Documents current state, design tokens, key file references, and risk mitigation strategies.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown documentation with embedded CSS snippets and phase-based implementation plan.

nodes.name: .vscode/extensions.json
nodes.summary: VS Code recommended extensions configuration. Recommends the Svelte language support extension for the project.
nodes.languageNotes: JSON configuration file.

nodes.name: .vscode/settings.json
nodes.summary: VS Code workspace settings file. Configures the Fallow coverage capture path for the project root.
nodes.languageNotes: JSON configuration file.

nodes.name: decode_full.mjs
nodes.summary: Standalone Node.js build decoder for testing and debugging. Reads game data JSON files (armors, rings, runes, blades, handles, races, guilds, enchantments, perks) and applies stat upgrades, enchantments, shrine multipliers, and infusion modifiers to compute accumulated stats and perks for a hardcoded test build state. Displays weapon damage types and core stat breakdowns.
nodes.languageNotes: ES module JavaScript file using readFileSync for synchronous file I/O. Mirrors stat/upgrade/enchantment logic from the main application's engine layer.

nodes.name: docs/ONBOARDING.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive onboarding guide for the voxlblade project. Documents all architecture layers (Root, Source, Data, Library, Constants, Engine, Modals, Statistics, Stores), key concepts including the damage calculation pipeline and engine architecture, and provides a guided 10-step tour with a complete file map.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown documentation with tables and structured reference sections.

nodes.name: src/app.css
nodes.summary: Global CSS stylesheet defining the design system foundation. Contains CSS custom properties for colors (surface, accent, weapon, draconic), spacing scale, border radius, shadows, typography, animations, z-index layers, glass morphism effects, and global resets including scrollbar styling and selection highlighting.
nodes.languageNotes: CSS with extensive custom property definitions across multiple :root blocks. Includes Phase 4.5 design tokens (spacing, radius, shadows, durations, typography) and component-specific utility classes (weapon art box, wild bolt box, scaling grid drag-and-drop targets).

nodes.name: src/App.svelte
nodes.summary: Root application component orchestrating the entire Voxel Blade damage calculator UI. Manages build state, equipment selection via modals, enchantment configuration, weapon stat calculations, draconic color/ability systems, tag-based stat filtering, armor drag-and-drop, tab navigation between overview and analysis views, and undo/redo for build clearing. Renders the complete interface including armor slots, weapon parts, rings, runes, race/guild selection, stat summary panels, and delegates to child components for damage analysis and buff management.
nodes.languageNotes: Svelte 5 component using $: reactive statements (not yet runes). ~3979 lines. Extensive imports from engine, store, data modules, and UI components. Manages complex state for modals, search suggestions, enchantment slots, and weapon stat filtering.

nodes.name: src/BaseDamageCalc.svelte
nodes.summary: Base damage calculation and display component. Computes per-hit damage breakdowns across multiple damage types with defense mitigation, applies typed boosts, armor penetration, crit calculations, debuff multipliers, proc effects (Echo Incineration, Luminescent, Lightning Cloak, Storm Rend, etc.), and displays results with interactive enemy HP slider and training dummy visualization. Handles DoT tick calculations, heal amounts, and Curse Rip mechanics.
nodes.languageNotes: Svelte 5 component (~2659 lines). Uses createEventDispatcher for parent communication. Heavy reactive logic for damage computation including armor penetration branching, debuff combat effects, and multiple proc effect calculations.

nodes.name: src/BuffList.svelte
nodes.summary: Buff and debuff management display component. Shows active buffs/debuffs from perks, items, weapon arts, and draconic sources with potency, duration, and effect details. Supports toggling individual buffs, warding debuff multiplier calculation, weapon proc coefficient tracking, and auto-debuff display from perks.
nodes.languageNotes: Svelte 5 component (~759 lines). Imports buff definitions, draconic buffs, boost calculations, weapon arts, and UI constants. Handles complex buff stacking and interaction rules.

nodes.name: src/BuildSaves.svelte
nodes.summary: Build save/load management component providing multiple save slots with localStorage persistence. Supports saving, loading, deleting, renaming builds, importing/exporting via JSON clipboard, and confirmation dialogs with timeout-based auto-dismiss.
nodes.languageNotes: Svelte 5 component (~596 lines). Uses store, toast notifications, constants for save key mapping, and BuildState type. Manages save slot array with localStorage read/write.

nodes.name: src/CdrStepsCalc.svelte
nodes.summary: Cooldown reduction step-by-step calculator display component. Shows the chain of CDR multiplier steps from base cooldown to final value, including set-bonus overrides.
nodes.languageNotes: Svelte 5 component (~93 lines). Simple display component with props for breakdown steps, base cooldown, and optional set CD override.

nodes.name: src/CritIcon.svelte
nodes.summary: Small SVG critical hit icon component. Renders a lightning bolt shape with a red-to-orange gradient, sized via prop.
nodes.languageNotes: Svelte 5 component (~14 lines). Pure presentational SVG with size prop and unique gradient ID.

nodes.name: src/DamageAnalyzer.svelte
nodes.summary: Primary damage analysis interface and the largest component in the application. Orchestrates comprehensive damage calculation including base damage, weapon hits, typed damage boosts, buff/debuff interactions, crit calculations, armor penetration, defense mitigation, heal calculations, DoT tick processing, perk on-hit effects, weapon art bonuses, draconic abilities, self-damage perks, stance overlays, and mounts. Delegates rendering to BaseDamageCalc and coordinates data from nearly every data module.
nodes.languageNotes: Svelte 5 component (~6864 lines). The heart of the damage calculation system. Imports from virtually every data module in the project. Contains the main damage pipeline logic connecting base stats, buffs, perks, weapon arts, and defensive mechanics.

nodes.name: src/data/Boost.ts
nodes.summary: Boost calculation logic defining damage multipliers for offensive perks including Frenzy, Minion Absorption, Blood Thirsty, Venom Spitter, Perfection, Stealth, Golden Crits, Royal Parry, Spell Piercer, Scourge, Sharpshooter, Valor, Gorecast, Undead Might, Highlander, Queen's Power, Venom Eater, Guardian Spin, Wild Bolt, Weighty Slam, Rider, Quickdraw, Spring Powered, Thief Training, and many more. Also defines the BoostContext interface and Frenzy/MinionAbsorption calculation functions.
nodes.languageNotes: TypeScript module (~426 lines). Exports calcMinionAbsorptionPotency, calcFrenzyPct, and BOOST_DEF_MAP with per-perk boost definitions. Heavy use of constant imports from lib/constants.

nodes.name: src/data/BuffData.ts
nodes.summary: Central buff and debuff definition system. Defines all buff/debuff effects with their potency calculations, duration modifiers, stacking rules, and interaction conditions. Handles buff granting from items, perks, weapon arts, and draconic sources. Includes tailwind-to-whirlwind conversion, toxin transfer duration extension, burn application counting, active buff assembly, and formatted perk description generation.
nodes.languageNotes: TypeScript module (~1874 lines). The largest data module. Exports BUFF_DEFS, getActiveBuildBuffs, getPerkBuffs, getWeaponArtBuffs, applyBuffPerkModifiers, calcBuffEffect, getBuffDescription, assembleActiveBuffs, and many helper functions. Imports extensively from lib/constants/buffs.

nodes.name: src/data/cdr.ts
nodes.summary: Cooldown reduction perk data definitions. Defines CDR effects for perks including Caster, Channeled Weapon, Mage Rage, Poison Acceleration, Runic Winds, Wave Rider, Whirlwind, Gladiatorial Rage, and Voltaic Body. Each entry specifies percentage reductions or multipliers for rune and weapon art cooldowns.
nodes.languageNotes: TypeScript module (~64 lines). Exports CDR_PERK_DATA record and PerkCDREntry interface. Imports CDR constants from lib/constants.

nodes.name: src/data/debuffCombatEffects.ts
nodes.summary: Debuff combat effect definitions. Defines the combat effects for debuffs including Weakness (damage reduction), Shatter and Electrical Rend (armor strip), Sticky variants (slow + type damage increase based on Melting Slime/Sickness/Hex Web), Despair (outgoing damage boost), Frostbite (water/air vulnerability), and Hypnotized (magic armor strip).
nodes.languageNotes: TypeScript module (~98 lines). Exports DEBUFF_COMBAT_EFFECTS record with descFn, damageMult, defReduction, and typeDamageMult per debuff.

nodes.name: src/data/defensivePerks.ts
nodes.summary: Defensive perk source definitions and calculations. Defines damage reduction sources from perks including Barb/Skin, Stoneskin, Adaptive Plate, Protector Spirit, Air Pressure, Valor, Vampire, Frenzy, Stored Corruption, Mounted Defense, Carapace, and Cursed Bark. Calculates total effective damage reduction from all active defensive sources with HP threshold and condition-based activation.
nodes.languageNotes: TypeScript module (~207 lines). Exports getActiveDefensivePerkSources and calcDefensivePotencyMult. Uses DefensivePerkSourceContext for conditional perk activation (HP-based, mounted, rage, etc.).

nodes.name: src/data/DoTDamage.ts
nodes.summary: Damage over time (DoT) calculation system. Defines base damage formulas, potency multipliers, and tick calculations for Bleed, Burn, and Poison DoT types. Includes Caustic Slow calculation with slow-duration scaling, builder-to-game potency conversion, and display potency computation with Endless Despair modifier.
nodes.languageNotes: TypeScript module (~103 lines). Exports getDotBase, getDotPotencyMult, calcDotTick, calcCausticSlow, toGamePotency, calcDotDisplayPotency, DOT_SCALINGS, DOT_DMG_TYPE_MAP.

nodes.name: src/data/draconicBuffs.ts
nodes.summary: Draconic guild buff system. Calculates Draconic Infusion buff potency and duration multipliers, generates Draconic Infusion buffs for each element color (air/fire/hex/holy/water/earth), and creates ability debuffs for Dragon Claw and Dragon Bubble (hex color gets random debuff pool, fire gets burn application).
nodes.languageNotes: TypeScript module (~112 lines). Exports getDraconicInfusionPotMult, getDraconicInfusionDurMult, getDraconicInfusionBuff, getDraconicAbilityDebuffs, getEffectiveDraconicInfusionPotency. Imports from BuffData for buff modification.

nodes.name: src/data/draconicColorEffects.ts
nodes.summary: Draconic color attack stat definitions. Defines the six draconic colors (fire/red, air/white, earth/green, water/blue, holy/yellow, hex/purple) with their damage multipliers, speed multipliers, knockback, cooldown, stun, and status effect properties. Provides getDraconicColorDmgMultiplier lookup function.
nodes.languageNotes: TypeScript module (~50 lines). Exports DRACONIC_COLOR_ATTACK_STATS array and getDraconicColorDmgMultiplier function. Uses DraconicColorAttackStats interface.

nodes.name: src/data/draconicRunes.ts
nodes.summary: Draconic rune damage bonus calculations. Applies color-specific damage type bonuses from Draconic Rune stacks (5% per stack of the selected color's damage type) and Dragon Infusion bonus (adds buff potency to the selected color's damage type). Provides both in-place and additive variants.
nodes.languageNotes: TypeScript module (~104 lines). Exports applyDraconicBonuses and getDraconicBonuses with context interfaces for stacks and infusion state.

nodes.name: src/data/enemyHpEffects.ts
nodes.summary: Enemy HP-gated perk effect definitions. Defines effects that activate based on enemy HP thresholds: Mycotic Bloom applies Slowness to poisoned enemies above 50% HP and grants +25% Poison DoT damage below 50% HP. Provides lookup functions for active HP-gated debuffs and DoT multipliers.
nodes.languageNotes: TypeScript module (~90 lines). Exports ENEMY_HP_EFFECT_DEFS, getActiveEnemyHpDebuffs, getEnemyHpDotMultiplier, and interface types.

nodes.name: src/data/HealBoost.ts
nodes.summary: Healing boost calculation system. Computes total healing multiplier from multiple sources including level scaling, Emotional perk (both buffs and debuffs active), Heal Boost perk, Oceans Rage, Vampire sunlight penalty, Frenzy self-healing reduction, and Dragon Infusion (Holy). Includes Anti Heal self-debuff reduction. Returns detailed breakdown entries and final multiplier.
nodes.languageNotes: TypeScript module (~216 lines). Exports calculateHealBoost function and HealBoostResult/HealBoostContext interfaces. Uses HealBoostDef pattern with calcFn callbacks for complex conditions.

nodes.name: src/data/mountRunes.ts
nodes.summary: Mount rune weapon definition data. Defines M1 attacks, Weapon Art attacks, damage types, scalings, and secondary effects for three mount runes: Winter Woof (physical, summon scaling, Shatter WA), Slizard (physical, 10-hit WA), and Glacial Snapper (water/physical hybrid).
nodes.languageNotes: TypeScript module (~89 lines). Exports MOUNT_RUNE_DEFS array and MountRuneDef/MountRuneM1Def/MountRuneWADef interfaces. Uses WeaponHit type from lib/types.

nodes.name: perkAutoDebuffs.ts
nodes.summary: Calculates automatic debuffs applied by perks (e.g., Burn from Exhaust, Bleed from Penance, Poison from Fungal Prototype). Exports getAutoDebuffs() which takes player state and returns an array of GrantedBuff debuff objects to apply on hit.
nodes.languageNotes: TypeScript. Imports constants from Boost, perks, perk-base-damage, game modules and types from lib/types. Contains ~15 perk-specific debuff branches with conditional logic.

nodes.name: Perkbasedmg.ts
nodes.summary: Central perk damage definition file. Defines PerkDmgDef interfaces and PERK_DMG_DEFS array containing ~30+ perk damage definitions (Springblast, Dragon State, Spore Burst, Venom Spitter, Dark Harvest, etc.) with base damage formulas, damage types, scaling modes, HP gates, and secondary effects.
nodes.languageNotes: TypeScript. Large file (864 lines) with many imported constants from lib/constants. Defines complex types (PerkDmgCtx, HpGate, SecondaryEffect, TriggerChainEntry) and helper functions (gateThreshold, isHpGateActive, calcSpringblastBaseDamage, calcBomberChargeBaseDamage).

nodes.name: procCoefficients.ts
nodes.summary: Defines proc coefficient lookup tables for weapons, weapon arts, runes, and perks. Maps weapon types to their M1/M2 proc coefficients and provides WA/rune/perk proc coefficient records for the proc chance system.
nodes.languageNotes: TypeScript. Pure data file with 4 exported Record objects (WEAPON_PROC_COEFFS, WA_PROC_COEFFS, RUNE_PROC_COEFFS, PERK_PROC_COEFFS) and 2 default constants.

nodes.name: raceEffects.ts
nodes.summary: Defines race-specific conditional effects, notably the Human race passive (damage boost and DR below 50% HP) and Ork Tenacity buff counting logic. Exports helper functions to get active race effects and calculate Ork Tenacity bonuses.
nodes.languageNotes: TypeScript. Imports HpGate and isHpGateActive from Perkbasedmg. Contains generic helper functions for Ork Tenacity buff filtering.

nodes.name: Runebasedmg.ts
nodes.summary: Defines rune damage definitions (RUNE_DMG_DEFS array) covering 12+ rune types (Ancient Cleric, Beenade, Boostshroom, Thunderous Charge, Sporeling Toss, Rocky Tail, etc.) with base damage formulas, damage types, scalings, hit counts, sliders, and shield definitions.
nodes.languageNotes: TypeScript. Imports from lib/types, HealBoost, SummonData, and many constants from rune-base-damage. Defines RuneDmgCtx, RuneSliderDef, RuneShieldDef, and RuneDmgDef interfaces.

nodes.name: selfDamagePerks.ts
nodes.summary: Defines self-damage perk definitions (Undead Might, Explosive Charge, Dark Magic, Bombardier) and the calcSelfDamage() function. Computes damage dealt to the player when using certain perks, including DR reduction and multi-target falloff.
nodes.languageNotes: TypeScript. Self-contained with no imports. Exports SelfDamagePerkDef interface, SELF_DAMAGE_PERK_DEFS array, and calcSelfDamage function with harmonic-series multi-target falloff.

nodes.name: stanceOverlays.ts
nodes.summary: Resolves weapon stance overlays based on equipped items and perks (e.g., Blaster Ring changes M2 to Rifle/Shotgun, Locked And Loaded changes to Dual Guns/Side Gun, Cosmic Ray/Mine overrides). Returns the overlay weapon type and scope.
nodes.languageNotes: TypeScript. Self-contained with no imports. Defines StanceOverlayResult, StanceOverlayCtx interfaces and resolveStanceOverlay() function with 6 overlay definitions.

nodes.name: statboost.ts
nodes.summary: Implements stat-transforming perk effects that modify player stats (e.g., Quick Witted converts speed to dex, Carapace converts earth to protection, Brawny converts elemental boosts to physical). Exports applyStatBoostPerks() which applies 16 perk handlers in a defined execution order.
nodes.languageNotes: TypeScript. Imports StatMap/StatKey types and many conversion constants. Defines a PERK_REGISTRY with 16 named handlers and PERK_EXECUTION_ORDER for deterministic processing.

nodes.name: SummonData.ts
nodes.summary: Defines summon creature data (Mage Bomber, Blessedling) with stats, damage types, and attacks. Provides calcSummonStat() for scaling with summon boost and level, calcMaxSummonCount() for cap calculations, and lookup maps for WA-to-summon and perk definitions.
nodes.languageNotes: TypeScript. Imports MAX_LEVEL from lib/constants. Defines SummonDef, SummonAttack, SummonPerkDef interfaces and exports SUMMON_MAP, WA_SUMMON_MAP lookup objects.

nodes.name: TypedDmgBoost.ts
nodes.summary: Calculates typed damage boosts from perks that scale specific damage types (Glyph Conduit boosts magic, Rage boosts physical+conditional, Photosynthesis boosts holy/earth in sunlight, Hex Shield boosts all types). Exports calcTypedDmgBoosts() returning per-type multipliers and active entries.
nodes.languageNotes: TypeScript. Imports GrantedBuff from BuffData and ALL_DMG_TYPES from damage-types constants. Defines TypedDmgBoostDef with getAffectedTypes/getDamageMultiplier callbacks.

nodes.name: weapon base dmg.ts
nodes.summary: Static data file defining WEAPON_BASE_DMG array with base M1/M2 damage values and hit patterns for 30+ weapon types (Fists, swords, spears, ranged weapons, etc.). Includes optional M2 charge mechanics for Rifle.
nodes.languageNotes: TypeScript. Imports WeaponBaseDmg type from lib/types. Pure data array with multi-hit notation ({n: dmg, count: hits}).

nodes.name: Weaponartcheck.ts
nodes.summary: Validates whether a weapon art can be equipped given current weapon scalings, stats, weapon type, and guild. Exports checkWA() for boolean validation and getUnmetReqs() for detailed unmet requirement strings. Handles scaling exemptions and weapon type equivalences.
nodes.languageNotes: TypeScript. Imports WeaponArt/WeaponArtRequirement types from weaponArts. Defines SCALE_MAP, STAT_CHECKS mappings and helper functions for scaling/stat requirement checks.

nodes.name: weaponArts.ts
nodes.summary: Comprehensive weapon art definitions (WEAPON_ARTS array) with 40+ weapon arts covering all elements (fire, water, earth, air, hex, holy) and guilds (Monk). Each entry includes name, description, cooldown, base damage, damage type, scaling, requirements, and extras.
nodes.languageNotes: TypeScript. No imports. Large data file (511 lines) defining WeaponArt, WeaponArtRequirement, WAIndividualHit interfaces. Contains extensive requirements objects with scaling/stat/weapon-type constraints.

nodes.name: weaponConditionalBoosts.ts
nodes.summary: Defines conditional damage multipliers that apply to specific weapon types and hit scopes (e.g., Aggressive Personality boosts War Hammer/Dual Mallets M1 finisher and Mallet M2). Exports getWeaponConditionalBoost() returning a multiplier and matching perk labels.
nodes.languageNotes: TypeScript. Imports roundMultiplier from lib/utils. Contains 2 boost definitions with weapon type and hit scope matching.

nodes.name: DidYouMean.svelte
nodes.summary: Svelte component displaying 'Did you mean?' suggestions when search yields no exact results. Renders clickable chips for suggested items (names or perks) with highlighted text matching, and a fallback empty-state message.
nodes.languageNotes: Svelte component using createEventDispatcher. Imports highlightTextParts utility. Styled with scoped CSS using accent3/accent2 color variables.

nodes.name: DmgTotalTooltip.svelte
nodes.summary: Svelte tooltip component that displays a detailed damage breakdown for a computed hit. Shows a donut pie chart of damage type distribution, grouped entries with values and badges, and percentage breakdowns. Uses Svelte 5 runes for reactivity.
nodes.languageNotes: Svelte 5 with $props, $derived, $state runes. Imports BADGE_CONFIG/ComputedHit from dmgTypes and Badge UI component. Contains SVG pie chart with CSS transitions.

nodes.name: DraconicAbilityStats.svelte
nodes.summary: Svelte component displaying Draconic Rune Infusion ability stats (damage type, scaling, poise damage, and optional holy/water heals) based on selected draconic color. Minimal presentational component.
nodes.languageNotes: Svelte component with exported props (dracoColor, poiseDmg, healHoly, healWater, showHoly, showWater). Minimal script, mostly template with scoped styles.

nodes.name: EmotionalTracker.svelte
nodes.summary: Svelte component for selecting the emotional state (buff/debuff/both) that affects gameplay calculations. Provides button toggles and a styled range slider with visual feedback (color, glow, border changes) tied to the build store's emotionalState field.
nodes.languageNotes: Svelte component using $: reactivity and build store. Imports Badge UI component. Features a custom-styled range slider with segmented visual track and animated dot indicators.

nodes.name: Highlight.svelte
nodes.summary: Minimal Svelte component that highlights matching text segments within a string based on a search query. Uses highlightTextParts utility and renders matches with <mark> elements.
nodes.languageNotes: Svelte component. Imports highlightTextParts from lib/utils. 16 lines, purely presentational.

nodes.name: LevelBar.svelte
nodes.summary: Interactive Svelte component for level and HP management. Displays an editable level input, a draggable HP fill bar with shield overlay, lost HP zone, threshold markers, and notches. Persists values to localStorage via the build store.
nodes.languageNotes: Svelte component using $: reactivity. Imports tick from svelte, build store, MAX_LEVEL/calcBaseMaxHP/STORAGE_KEY constants, and Badge UI. Complex mouse drag handling, HP calculation from protection, and extensive CSS animations.

nodes.name: AppHeader.svelte
nodes.summary: Application header component with the VoxlBuilder branding, hint text, animated tab switcher (Overview/Analyze) with sliding bubble indicator, and embedded BuildSaves component.
nodes.languageNotes: Svelte component using createEventDispatcher, tick, onMount. Imports BuildSaves component. Features animated tab bubble with CSS transitions and responsive breakpoints.

nodes.name: constants.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel re-export file that re-exports everything from the constants/index module, serving as the single entry point for all game constants used throughout the project.
nodes.languageNotes: TypeScript. Single line: export * from './constants/index'. Acts as a namespace aggregator for the constants subdirectory.

nodes.name: boosts.ts
nodes.summary: Defines numeric constants for damage boost multipliers and complex boost calculations (Frenzy, Blood Thirsty, Perfection, Scourge, Valor, Highlander, Emotional, etc.). Includes both simple per-stack multipliers and internal values for complex calcFn implementations.
nodes.languageNotes: TypeScript. Pure data file with ~58 exported numeric constants. No imports, no functions.

nodes.name: buffs.ts
nodes.summary: Defines buff/debuff potency and duration constants used across the game's buff system. Covers rune-granted buffs, weapon-art buffs, perk buffs (Perfection, Bounce, Springblast, etc.), True Balance conversions, and generic debuff modifiers.
nodes.languageNotes: TypeScript. Pure data file with ~152 exported numeric constants organized into thematic sections. No imports, no functions.

nodes.name: cdr.ts
nodes.summary: Defines cooldown reduction (CDR) constants for various perks and abilities (Caster, Channeled Weapon, Mage Rage, Poison Acceleration, Runic Winds, Wave Rider, Whirlwind, etc.).
nodes.languageNotes: TypeScript. Pure data file with ~15 exported numeric constants. No imports, no functions.

nodes.name: damage-types.ts
nodes.summary: Defines damage type metadata including labels, colors, defense inheritance mappings, priority ordering, and type constant arrays for all 11 damage types in the game.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure TypeScript with Record and readonly array exports

nodes.name: debuff-effects.ts
nodes.summary: Defines numerical constants for debuff combat effects including weakness formula values, defense conversion rates, sticky damage multipliers, and despair damage coefficients.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure numeric constant exports

nodes.name: defensive.ts
nodes.summary: Defines damage reduction (DR) values for all defensive perks including barbskin, stoneskin, adaptive plate, protector spirit, air pressure, valor shield, vampire, frenzy, stored corruption, mounted defense, carapace, and cursed bark.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure numeric constant exports with fractional values

nodes.name: dot-damage.ts
nodes.summary: Defines damage-over-time (DoT) constants including base damage, infusion divisor, type scaling factors for bleed/burn/poison/caustic, potency caps, rounding factor, and builder-to-game conversion.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure numeric constant exports

nodes.name: draconic-colors.ts
nodes.summary: Defines draconic color-specific multipliers for elemental effects including fire speed, air speed/knockback/cooldown, earth damage/speed/stun, water damage/speed/stun, and hex stun/random debuff chances.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure numeric constant exports

nodes.name: draconic.ts
nodes.summary: Defines draconic infusion potency/duration multipliers, dragon state HP thresholds and base values, and bastion blessing multiplier.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure numeric constant exports

nodes.name: game.ts
nodes.summary: Core game constants including max/default level, HP formulas with calcBaseMaxHP function, and UI timing constants for save debounce, undo window, search blur, import success display, focus delay, and toast duration.
nodes.languageNotes: Constants with one exported helper function calcBaseMaxHP

nodes.name: heals.ts
nodes.summary: Defines critical healing constants including base healing value, holy boost divisor, per-stack healing bonus, and ocean song per-stack multiplier.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure numeric constant exports

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel file that re-exports all constants modules including damage-types, game, storage, thresholds, perks, boosts, heals, draconic, perk-base-damage, rune-base-damage, buffs, stat-conversions, defensive, dot-damage, draconic-colors, cdr, and debuff-effects.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure re-export barrel file

nodes.name: perk-base-damage.ts
nodes.summary: Defines base damage values for all damage-dealing perks including dragon claw, dragon bubble, springblast, various spirits (basic, buni, mageling, toadzerker, dire buni, void root, roguent), bounce momentum, protector spirit, air pressure/barrier, roaring heads, barbed flurry, honey arts, sickness, melting slime, whirlwind, volatile shell, royal finisher, grounded despair, echo incineration, venom spitter, spore burst, apollo boost, HP gates, bomber charge, and dark harvest.
nodes.languageNotes: 150 lines of numeric constants organized by perk name with comments

nodes.name: perks.ts
nodes.summary: Defines perk-related constants including damage multipliers for luminescent, spirit winds, dark magic, wind walker, reaper, extinguish, explosive charge, wild bolt, lightning cloak, curse rip, true balance, emotional, toxin transfer, void rage, channeled weapon, draconic blood; crit base values; perk crit-rate multipliers; crit damage formulas; and special perk mechanics for mortal will, sunburn, frostbite, fiery pursuit, hypnotist, and phantom pain.
nodes.languageNotes: 88 lines with organized sections and comments

nodes.name: rune-base-damage.ts
nodes.summary: Defines base damage values for all rune abilities including ancient cleric, beenade, boostshroom, thunderous charge, sporeling toss, foot dive, caci, cacitrops, hex web, brainblast, and rocky tail with its complex scaling formula constants.
nodes.languageNotes: 54 lines of numeric constants organized by rune name

nodes.name: stat-conversions.ts
nodes.summary: Defines stat boost conversion rates for various perks including weight distribution, quick witted, whirl foot, carapace, extra layers, frozen heart, immovable, righted wrongs, rocky body, spellshield, strong tides, swift guard, true balance, rawny, and subtraction modifiers for roaring headings, lucky, and other perks.
nodes.languageNotes: 30 lines of numeric conversion constants

nodes.name: storage.ts
nodes.summary: Defines localStorage keys for build data, defense data, level, HP fill, and saves; build state defaults with all equipment slots; save key mapping/unmapping for compact serialization; enchant slot mapping; and max build slots/confirm timeout.
nodes.languageNotes: 39 lines with Record types and Object.fromEntries for reverse mappings

tour.title: Project Setup & Bootstrap
tour.description: The application boots from a standard Svelte + Vite scaffold. package.json defines the build scripts (dev, build, preview) and lists Svelte 5, Vite 8, and TypeScript as core dependencies. Vite is configured in vite.config.ts with the Svelte plugin and a PWA plugin for offline support and service worker registration. The entry point main.ts is minimal: it imports global CSS, injects Vercel analytics, and mounts the root App component to the DOM.

tour.title: Type System Foundation
tour.description: All game entities are modeled through TypeScript interfaces in types.ts. This file defines StatKey (the union of all stat names like physicalBoost, fireDefense, etc.), StatMap (a partial record of stat values), and the core data shapes: Race, Guild, Armor, Ring, Rune, Enchantment, and Perk. It also defines WeaponBlade, WeaponHandle, MonkGlove, and MonkEssence types with their scaling and damage type properties. The BuildState interface captures every editable field in a build, from race and guild to individual equipment slots and enchantment configurations. This file is the single source of truth for the type contracts used across the entire engine and UI.

tour.title: Constants & Game Data
tour.description: The constants barrel file re-exports over a dozen focused constant modules covering damage types, game thresholds, CDR formulas, boost values, healing, draconic mechanics, and stat conversions. The game data layer lives in src/data/ and contains JSON datasets for every equipment category: blades.json and handles.json define weapon parts with stats and scaling values, armors.json holds armor sets with per-slot stats, enchantments.json catalogs all enchantment modifiers, and perks.json contains 365 perk definitions with tags and descriptions. TypeScript data modules like weaponArts.ts, BuffData.ts, Boost.ts, and Perkbasedmg.ts encode the game's calculation formulas and buff/debuff definitions.

tour.title: Centralized State Management
tour.description: The store.ts file creates the application's reactive state using Svelte's writable and derived stores. A single writable<BuildState> store holds all build selections, loaded from localStorage on startup and debounced-saved on every change. A derived store automatically recomputes the full build result (stats, perks, CDR, boosts, crit) whenever the build state changes by calling calcBuild. Helper functions like setEnchantment, setGuild, moveArmorSlot, and swapRingWithInfusion provide typed mutations that update the store atomically. This is the bridge between user interactions and the calculation engine.

tour.title: Weapon Calculation Engine
tour.description: weapon.ts resolves the final weapon from a blade + handle (or glove + essence for Monk guilds). It extracts damage types and scaling values from both parts, merges their perks and attack speeds, and detects hybrid weapons (where parts contribute different scaling types) to apply a 1.5x scaling multiplier. A lookup table maps handle type + blade type combinations to weapon types (e.g., Medium Blade + Long Handle = 2-Handed Sword), and perk-driven overrides transform the base type into special weapons like Dual Wielding Daggers or Rapier. The calcWeapon and calcMonkWeapon functions return a rich WeaponResult object containing merged stats, scalings, damage types, and per-part breakdowns.

tour.title: Enchantment Application
tour.description: enchant.ts implements the two-phase enchantment system. Phase 1 applies selector-based modifiers (Restored, Flawless) that transform base stats using predicates like 'positive stats' or 'defense stats'. Phase 2 applies direct stat modifiers (Quenched, Strengthened) as multipliers and flat additions, plus enchantment effects that add or scale perks. Upgrades are applied to the modified base between phases, with armor penetration kept exempt. The enforceEnchantSlot function ensures exclusive enchantments (like Ascended variants) occupy the slot alone, and applyPerkEffectiveness scales all perks by a multiplier derived from Perk Effectiveness and Cursed stacks.

tour.title: Full Build Calculation Pipeline
tour.description: build.ts is the heart of the calculation engine. The calcBuild function orchestrates the entire pipeline: accumulateEquipment collects stats and perks from race, guild, armor (with enchantments and upgrades), ring, rune, and weapon. finalizePerks applies Perk Effectiveness scaling, merges Buckler into Parry, and grants potency perks to their parent tags. deriveResults then computes the final stat totals, applies stat boost perks, calculates CDR (cooldown reduction) from perks and race modifiers, runs the crit calculation, assembles active buffs, and evaluates all damage boosts. The result is a BuildResult containing final stats, perks, CDR breakdown, boost breakdown, and crit data.

tour.title: Buff, Boost & Crit Systems
tour.description: BuffData.ts defines every buff and debuff in the game with their effects per tenth potency, colors, and conditions. It computes active buffs from equipment, perks, weapon arts, and draconic sources, applying perk-based modifiers and duration scaling. Boost.ts defines damage multipliers from perks like Frenzy, Perfection, and Valor, using either simple multiplier-per-perk formulas or complex context-dependent calculations. crit.ts computes critical hit chance from multiple independent sources (Dexterity, Flowing Crits, Sharp Crits, Perfection) using the formula 1 - product(1 - chance) for independent probabilities, and tracks crit damage multipliers from sources like Thief Training and Vital Strikes. defense.ts resolves defense sources into percent and flat multipliers, computing true damage reduction.

tour.title: Reusable UI Component Library
tour.description: The ui/ directory contains small, composable Svelte 5 components using runes ($props) for configuration. Badge.svelte renders inline labels with color variants (green, amber, red, purple, blue, teal, muted, draco), size options (xs, sm, md), and shape modifiers (square, solid, tag). Card.svelte provides a container with variant styles (default, surface, ghost), accent borders matching the badge colors, configurable padding/gap, and optional hover interaction. These components form the design system used throughout the application.

tour.title: Damage Analysis & Breakdown UI
tour.description: DamageAnalyzer.svelte (6,800+ lines) is the primary damage analysis interface. It reads the build store and result, then computes per-hit damage breakdowns by combining weapon base damage, scaling multipliers, buff effects, crit multipliers, defense reduction, and armor penetration. BaseDamageCalc.svelte renders the detailed hit-by-hit damage table with typed damage columns, crit overlays, and proc coefficient scaling. BuffList.svelte displays all active buffs and debuffs with their potency, duration, and effect descriptions, pulling from BuffData.ts to compute real-time buff interactions.

tour.title: Root Application & Orchestration
tour.description: App.svelte is the root component that wires everything together. It imports the build store and result, renders modal-based selection for race, guild, armor, weapons, and accessories, manages drag-and-drop for armor slot reordering, handles enchantment selection with inline editors, and provides search with fuzzy matching (Levenshtein distance). The component computes derived values like weapon result, stat rows, perk rows, and damage type breakdowns, then passes them to child components. Two main tabs switch between the overview (equipment, stats, perks) and the damage analyzer. An undo system preserves the previous build state for a brief window after clearing.

tour.title: Deployment & CI/CD
tour.description: The project deploys as a static site via GitHub Actions (static.yml) to Vercel. Vite's PWA plugin generates a service worker with cache-first strategies for static assets and stale-while-revalidate for navigation. The build output is a single-page application served from an index.html shell, with the base path configurable via VITE_BASE_PATH for hosting under subdirectories.
